title: 🤖 Copilot Adoption & Usage [v2]
description: Tracks GitHub Copilot seat provisioning, activation rates, and daily active user trends across the organization.

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'live' THEN '📡 Live Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo Environment — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    ELSE '❓ Unknown Mode'
  END AS "Data Source"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content:
# 🤖 Copilot Adoption & Usage

**Supplementary Metrics — Copilot Seat Utilisation**

## What it Measures
This dashboard tracks how broadly and actively GitHub Copilot is being used across your organisation. It covers seat allocation, activation rates, and 28-day active usage — giving you a clear picture of Copilot adoption health.

## Why it Matters
Seat utilisation directly affects ROI. Inactive seats represent wasted spend and missed productivity opportunities. Understanding *who* is using Copilot and *how often* helps managers target enablement efforts and coaches identify developers who may need onboarding support.

## How to Interpret
| Metric | Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|---|
| **Seat Utilisation Rate** | ≥80% active in 28d | <50% — investigate blockers or low awareness |
| **Inactive Seats (>14d)** | Close to 0 | Rising count — reach out to inactive users |
| **Feature Adoption** | Multiple surfaces used | Single surface — expand use-case awareness |

## How to Take Action
- **Low utilisation**: Run Copilot enablement sessions, share use-case demos, create internal champions
- **Many inactive seats**: Survey inactive users to understand blockers (access issues, workflow friction, low awareness)
- **Single-surface usage**: Encourage exploration of Chat, CLI, and PR Summary in addition to code completion

panels.description: Copilot Adoption & Usage — seat utilisation, active users, and feature adoption.

panels.title: Total Seats
panels.description:
### What it measures
Total number of Copilot seats provisioned in the organization.

### What insight it offers
Provides the baseline for calculating utilization rate. Compare with active seats to understand deployment effectiveness.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Includes all seats regardless of activity
- May include seats for service accounts or bots

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Total Seats" FROM copilot_seats

panels.title: Active Seats (28d)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Number of seats with recorded Copilot activity in the last 28 days.

### What insight it offers
Active seat count represents real adoption. Tracking this over time shows whether adoption is growing, stable, or declining.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Activity threshold is any usage recorded in last_activity_at
- Does not distinguish between heavy and light users

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Active Seats (28d)" FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.title: Seat Utilization Rate (%)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Percentage of provisioned seats that were actively used in the last 28 days.

### What insight it offers
Low utilization rates indicate wasted license spend or insufficient onboarding. Target above 80% for healthy adoption.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Based on last_activity_at from the seat record, not daily metrics
- New seats provisioned in the last 28 days may not yet show activity

panels.title: Inactive Seats (>14d)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Number of seats with no recorded activity for more than 14 days.

### What insight it offers
Highlights seats that may need re-engagement or reassignment. Reducing inactive seats improves ROI on Copilot licenses.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- 14-day threshold is configurable by changing the interval in the query
- Includes seats that may be for developers on leave

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Inactive Seats (>14d)"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '14 days'
   OR last_activity_at IS NULL

panels.title: Daily Active Users
panels.description:
### What it measures
Daily count of unique Copilot users with at least one suggestion interaction, from org-level metrics.

### What insight it offers
Shows daily engagement trends. Dips may correlate with holidays, incidents, or reduced developer availability.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Sourced from copilot_org_metrics; aggregated at org level
- Does not break down by team or repository

targets.rawSql:
SELECT day AS time, daily_active_users AS "Daily Active"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

panels.title: Last Active Editor Distribution
panels.description:
### What it measures
Breakdown of Copilot seats by the IDE/editor reported in their last activity.

### What insight it offers
Shows which editors are most popular among Copilot users, informing where to focus onboarding and support efforts.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Reports last known editor, not historical distribution
- Seats with no editor recorded are excluded

targets.rawSql:
SELECT last_activity_editor AS "Editor", COUNT(*) AS "Seats"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_editor IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

panels.title: Seat Activity Recency
panels.description:
### What it measures
Per-developer Copilot seat activity showing last active date, editor, and plan type.

### What insight it offers
Enables team leads to identify inactive developers for re-engagement and verify plan assignments are correct.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Ordered by most recently active; scroll to find inactive developers
- last_activity_at is updated by GitHub's API sync cadence

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  assignee_login AS "Developer",
  last_activity_at AS "Last Active",
  last_activity_editor AS "Editor",
  plan_type AS "Plan"
FROM copilot_seats
ORDER BY last_activity_at DESC

panels.title: Copilot Seats Loaded
panels.description: Total rows in the copilot_seats table. Shows 0 in red when the Copilot data pipeline has not run successfully — check sync job logs for HTTP 403/404 warnings.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats

panels.title: Last Successful Sync
panels.description: Timestamp of the most recent sync job that completed with status=success. If this is empty or very old, trigger a new sync: POST http://localhost:3003/api/sync

targets.rawSql: SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'
